content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN points (x1,y1),(x2,y2),‚Ä¶,(xN,yN)(x_1, y_1), (x_2, y_2), \ldots, (x_N, y_N)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã),(x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã),‚Ä¶,(xN‚Äã,yN‚Äã).

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given a range [f,t][f, t][f,t]. Consider the points (xf,yf),(xf+1,yf+1),‚Ä¶,(xt,yt)(x_f, y_f), (x_{f+1}, y_{f+1}), \ldots, (x_t, y_t)(xf‚Äã,yf‚Äã),(xf+1‚Äã,yf+1‚Äã),‚Ä¶,(xt‚Äã,yt‚Äã); the answer to a query is the square of the maximum Euclidean distance between any two of these points.
constraints: 0‚â§xi,yi‚â§2‚ãÖ1050 \le x_i, y_i \le 2 \cdot 10^50‚â§xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã‚â§2‚ãÖ105 for each valid iii
**Subtask #2 (35 points):** the coordinates of all points are chosen uniformly randomly, **TL=5s**
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi‚Äã and yiy_iyi‚Äã.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers fff and ttt describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the squared maximum distance.
